title: Research Workflows Operator Lesson
objective: Understand the operator fundamentals behind research workflows in strategy.
beginnerExplanation: Research Workflows becomes easier when you break it into signal, decisions, owners, and follow-through. This lesson gives you a practical structure instead of abstract advice.
deeperExplanation: At higher levels, research workflows is about tradeoffs. You are deciding where to focus, what to ignore, and how to keep the work legible enough for other people to trust.
keyTakeaways: Research Workflows improves when the operating goal is explicit.
You need evidence, not just motion, to judge whether research workflows is working.
Research Workflows should leave behind reusable assets like notes, decisions, scorecards, or systems.
example: A founder or operator uses research workflows to clarify what changed, what matters next, and what must happen before the next review.
commonMistakes: Treating research workflows like isolated activity instead of part of a larger system.
Skipping the review loop and assuming the first answer is good enough.
Letting ownership stay vague after the decision is made.
exercise: Write a one-page note explaining how you would improve research workflows over the next two weeks inside a real business.
